summary: Executed deployment-readiness regression on public preview URL for SSR pages, API routes, and chatbot flow after Supabase migration. Public pages and chatbot flow work, but required Next API readiness endpoints are returning 404.

critical.issue: Returns 404 on public preview URL instead of expected {status:'ok'} response.
Returns 404 on public preview URL, blocking verification that public content is served from migrated PostgreSQL runtime.

integration_issues.flow: Public content API verification after migration
integration_issues.issue: Cannot validate DB-backed public content through same-origin /api because required Next API routes are not exposed at runtime.

action_items: Fix runtime routing so Next route handlers are reachable on public domain: /api/health and /api/pages/public must return 200.
After routing fix, retest /api/pages/public payload for expected published records including '/' and 'contact' to confirm PostgreSQL-backed runtime data.
Investigate Prisma P1001 log pattern (DB host 127.0.0.1:1 observed in frontend supervisor logs) because it indicates possible hidden DB connectivity fallback path despite SSR 200 responses.
critical_code_review_comments: Public pages can still render 200 even if DB queries fail because page services catch DB errors and fall back to hardcoded content; this can mask migration/runtime DB outages.
Current preview ingress appears to route /api to external backend service only, making Next API handlers non-testable from public URL and breaking stated readiness checks.
seed_data_creation: Used transient session_id TEST_deployment_readiness for chatbot API smoke call.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on https://greeters-preview.preview.emergentagent.com. / and /contact return 200 and chatbot UI responds. Blocking gap: /api/health and /api/pages/public return 404 from public URL. Re-run pytest and playwright after routing fix.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: call GET https://greeters-preview.preview.emergentagent.com/api/health and GET /api/pages/public -> both 404. Expected behavior from migrated Next app is 200 with JSON payload. Likely cause: ingress/proxy still forwarding /api to FastAPI backend instead of Next route handlers, so Next API readiness endpoints are not publicly exposed. Secondary risk from logs: repeated Prisma P1001 (DB unreachable 127.0.0.1:1) can be hidden by fallback rendering paths on SSR pages.
